title: sphenostylis erecta subsp. obtusifolia
intro: Sphenostylis erecta subsp. obtusifolia, also known as the Bush Pumpkin, is a type of flowering plant native to tropical Africa. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The Bush Pumpkin has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and providing antioxidant protection. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
